title: Revisiting the validity of eddy viscosity models for predicting airflow over water waves
abstract: Examines the validity of eddy viscosity models for predicting turbulent airflow over water waves using high-fidelity LES. Provides theoretical and numerical assessment of model assumptions in the context of marine atmospheric boundary layer flows.

title: Wind farm dynamics over a diurnal cycle: Analysis of a comprehensive large-eddy simulation, web-services accessible dataset
journal: Journal of Renewable and Sustainable Energy
abstract: Analysis of wind farm flow dynamics over a full diurnal cycle using a comprehensive LES dataset accessible via web services. Investigates how the time-varying atmospheric boundary layer influences wake evolution, turbine interactions, and wind farm power output.

title: JHTDB-wind: a web-accessible large-eddy simulation database of a wind farm with virtual sensor querying
abstract: Presents JHTDB-wind, a publicly accessible web-services database of high-fidelity wind farm LES data with virtual sensor querying capabilities. Enables researchers worldwide to access turbulence and wake data without performing their own large-scale simulations.

title: Effect of swell-induced pitching motion on power production and wake dynamics of a floating offshore wind turbine at various wave ages
abstract: Investigates how swell-induced pitching motion affects power production and wake dynamics of a floating offshore wind turbine across different wave ages. Reveals the coupled effects of platform motion and wave age on turbine performance and downstream wake recovery.

title: An extended analytical wake model and applications to yawed wind turbines in atmospheric boundary layers with different levels of stratification and veer
journal: Journal of Renewable and Sustainable Energy
abstract: Develops an extended analytical wake model that accounts for wind veer and thermal stratification effects on yawed wind turbine wakes. The model accurately predicts wake deflection and shape under a wide range of atmospheric boundary layer conditions and is validated against LES data.

title: Analytical model coupling Ekman and surface layer structure in atmospheric boundary layer flows
abstract: Presents an analytical model that couples the Ekman layer and surface layer structure to predict wind veer, wind speed profiles, and geostrophic drag in atmospheric boundary layer flows. Provides a self-consistent framework applicable to wind farm modeling and resource assessment.

title: Analytical wake modeling in atmospheric boundary layers: accounting for wind veer and thermal stratification
abstract: Conference paper presenting an analytical wake model that accounts for wind veer and thermal stratification effects in atmospheric boundary layers, with applications to yawed wind turbine wake prediction.

title: Effects of wind veer on a yawed wind turbine wake in atmospheric boundary layer flow
abstract: Large Eddy Simulation (LES) is used to study the wind veer effects on the wake of a yawed wind turbine. The veer deflects the wake in the spanwise direction, and this effect can be captured in the wake model by adding a veer correction term. The counter-rotating vortex pair structures previously observed behind yawed turbines can also be recovered by subtracting the background veer vorticity.

title: Large eddy simulation of transitional channel flow using a machine learning classifier to distinguish laminar and turbulent regions
abstract: Bypass and subcritical orderly transition simulated using wall-modeled LES. Self-Organizing Map (SOM) distinguishes Turbulent-Non-Turbulent regions, applying wall-modeling selectively in turbulent zones. Successfully captures friction Reynolds number evolution at DNS-level accuracy with significantly reduced computational cost.

title: Damping of magnetohydrodynamic waves in a rotating fluid
abstract: Theoretical and numerical study of the damping mechanisms of magnetohydrodynamic (MHD) waves in a rapidly rotating fluid, with implications for wave dynamics in Earth's outer core.
